headline: Zen King West (2024) — 200m INDOOR RUNNING TRACK + 5000sqft gym + cold plunge spa
why_top: Unicorn-tier for a 100km/wk runner. Indoor track means winter mileage doesn't drop. Cold plunge spa = recovery for night-shift body. CentreCourt 2024 build = newest on the list. Walk Score 91, Transit 96.
watch_out: CentreCourt builds tight — at $2,150 you're in a 450sqft studio-1BR. Verify floor plan. Building is in occupancy phase 2024-25, expect 12mo of management teething. ASK: which units have access to the indoor track at peak hours? Is the track 24h?

building.name: ZEN King West
building.property_manager: Likely Crossbridge or Del (CentreCourt's usual rotation); confirm at viewing
building.pm_rep: CentreCourt is a polarizing dev — known for tight units, premium amenities, on-time delivery. Build quality reviews trending positive in early occupancy.
amenities: 3,500 sq ft Scandinavian spa (hot + cold plunge pools)
200m INDOOR running track (rare, big deal for a 100km/week runner)
red_flags: Small floorplate per unit — at $2,700 budget you may end up in 450-500 sqft
Newest building = early-occupancy mgmt teething likely over next 12mo

headline: Musée Condos (Plaza 2017) — best risk-adjusted King West pick
why_top: Plaza build > Freed/Lamb. FirstService PM (good rep). Outdoor rooftop pool. ONE block off King's loudest stretch = quieter for shift worker but still 8-min walk to King W bars. 2017 vintage = no Urbancorp concerns.
watch_out: AVOID low floor + pool/courtyard-facing (specific complaint: garbage truck noise early AM). Want higher floor, Adelaide-facing. ASK to see specifically how much sun the unit gets — Adelaide-facing > pool-courtyard.

building.name: Musée Condos
building.pm_rep: Plaza is one of the top-tier Toronto developers — 'buildings by Plaza like Ivory on Adelaide and Musée have been positive' (UrbanToronto). FirstService is large + reliable.
amenities: Rooftop patio with sweeping city views
red_flags: LOW-floor + pool-side = early-morning garbage truck noise (specific repeated complaint)
Location on Bathurst not King — slightly further walk to King West core (though still walkable)

headline: Kingly Condos (Allied + RioCan 2019) — strongest developer pedigree on the list
why_top: Allied Properties REIT + RioCan = institutional landlords, no condo board BS. Hariri Pontarini architect (premium). 132-unit BOUTIQUE feel — no elevator hell. Heritage brick podium aesthetic. Designed to last.
watch_out: No pool. Smaller gym vs Musée. 132 units = thinner amenity menu. NO research from Agent on this specific unit so verify. ASK: any short-term-rental units in building? What's the noise-transmission rating?

building.name: Kingly Condos (501 Adelaide + 620 King W)
building.developer: Allied Properties REIT + RioCan REIT (designed by Hariri Pontarini Architects)
building.property_manager: Allied Properties / FirstService (Allied retains hands-on involvement on their projects)
building.pm_rep: Allied is a publicly-traded REIT specializing in heritage-conversion + high-quality urban product; their PM oversight is reputationally strong. Newer building means PM track record still forming.
building.size: 132–133 units over 15 storeys (550–1300 sqft, plus larger penthouse-grade)
red_flags: Smallest unit count on the list (132 units) = thinner amenities, less elevator redundancy
Newer = less long-run mechanicals track record (but also less wear)

headline: Minto 775 King West (LEED Gold 2013) — Loblaws + Shoppers IN PODIUM, $1,900
why_top: Minto = top-tier dev quality. LEED Gold. Loblaws + Shoppers DrugMart literally in the building (grocery walk = 30 seconds). Walk Score 93, Transit 100, Bike 95. At $1,900 this is the lowest-priced unit in the top tier.
watch_out: AVOID inner-courtyard units (dark, cramped — picking wrong floorplan kills the deal). Want 17-storey south or west-facing. King-facing units at low floors get streetcar morning noise. ASK to see floorplan + which direction the unit faces BEFORE viewing.
alt_unit: None in dataset (only one unit here)

building.name: Minto 775 King West
building.pm_rep: Minto is one of the higher-reputation builder/PM combos in Toronto — generally well-managed.
red_flags: Layouts vary wildly — picking the wrong floorplan = dark unit facing inner court
King-facing units at streetcar level get morning noise

headline: Liberty Place West (2015) — south-facing lake views, solid mid-tier Liberty
why_top: 2015 build = no Urbancorp era. Full amenities (gym, pool). South-facing units = lake views. Mid-tier price for Liberty. Walk to Liberty Village Striders run club at Aroma (Lynn Williams) = 6 min.
watch_out: ASK about which floor faces SOUTH (lake) vs NORTH (rail corridor). North-facing = train noise. 1 documented red flag but not severe. Verify the actual unit's view + sun direction at viewing.

building.name: Liberty Central by the Lake (Phase I)
building.property_manager: Crossbridge / FirstService Residential (per Liberty Village condo manager directory)
building.pm_rep: FirstService acquired Crossbridge April 2023; among the more competent Liberty PMs but high volume across LV portfolio means inconsistent unit-level response.
building.size: approx 385-395 units / 25 floors (twin curved glass towers)
red_flags: No major systemic red flags surfaced. Generic large-tower friction (elevator peaks, move-in chaos) noted.

run_clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs.meets: Tue 7pm Stay Gold, Sat 9am Bather (450 Dufferin)

amenities_nearby.best_run_loop: South to Coronation Park → Martin Goodman Trail (6 min). Humber Bay loop = 12km; High Park via Queen W = ~10km loop. Liberty Village → Parkdale → Roncesvalles is a classic 8km social run.
amenities_nearby.running_insight: Same as the other Liberty Village picks — bullseye on East Liberty puts you on top of F45, Metro, and the LV Striders meetup. 100km/week is structurally easy.

headline: Liberty Walk (2013) — boutique 201-unit, quieter Liberty Village
why_top: 201 units = 50%+ smaller than the East Liberty tri-tower complex. Means: faster elevators, quieter amenity spaces, less Airbnb. Lynn Williams is the quietest Liberty street (off main drag). Liberty Village Striders MEETS HERE (Aroma at 120 Lynn Williams).
watch_out: Thinner amenity package vs tri-tower (no bowling alley, no virtual golf). If you don't care about big amenity stack, this is the better quiet pick.
alt_unit: None more in dataset (one of two on Lynn Williams)

building.name: Liberty on the Park
building.pm_rep: Smaller building = generally better unit-level service.
red_flags: Amenity-light vs others on the list — no pool, no sauna, no big lounge

amenities_nearby.best_run_loop: South under rail corridor to Coronation Park (5 min) onto Martin Goodman Trail. West to Humber Bay Arch = 10-12km round trip; east to CityPlace = 6km. Liberty Village → Parkdale → High Park loop = 8km classic.
amenities_nearby.running_insight: Best of both worlds: in the Liberty Village run-club epicenter (Striders + Parkdale RR + F45 all <20 min) AND 5 min to the lake. King & Bathurst nightlife is a hike (20 min) — Uber it.

headline: King West Condominiums Phase III (Plaza 2013) — bowling alley + virtual golf + sky lounge
why_top: Plaza build. Crossbridge/FirstService PM. SHARED amenity complex with 75 + 85 East Liberty: bowling alley, virtual golf, sky lounge, indoor pool, movie theatre. Genuinely impressive for dating ('come over, we have a bowling alley'). Ossington bus from front door.
watch_out: 425 units = elevator congestion at peak. 75 East Liberty same complex is slightly smaller (300 units) so consider that as an alternative. ASK: how many units per floor? Floor heights? Is the pool actually open year-round?
alt_unit: 75 East Liberty #1010 ($2,750) — same complex, slightly smaller, same amenities

building.name: King West Condominiums Phase III (Liberty Lakeview Towers complex w/ 75 & 85)
building.property_manager: Crossbridge / FirstService Residential (shared mgmt across the King West tri-tower per LV condo directory)
building.pm_rep: Plaza buildings generally well-regarded for construction quality; mgmt criticism is that ONE property manager covers nearly 1,200 suites across three towers — response time can lag.
amenities: billiards / games room (ping pong, foosball, air hockey)

headline: Yards at Fort York (Onni 2015) — best 'quiet professional' Fort York pick
why_top: Onni Group = top-tier dev. FirstService PM. 5-year residents specifically called out SOUNDPROOFING as good. Has a real squat rack in the gym. Fort York west of Bathurst (Onni/Context/Conservatory builds) is genuinely more mature than Concord CityPlace east of Bathurst.
watch_out: No pool. ASK to specifically test sound transmission (knock on adjacent unit walls during viewing). Verify which floor — Fort York Blvd noise on low floors.

building.name: The Yards at Fort York
building.pm_rep: FirstService is widely considered the best of the big-three Toronto condo PMs; Onni QA is significantly higher than Concord Adex.
red_flags: right next to Gardiner Expressway - soundproofing reportedly handles it, but ask to visit during rush hour
smaller amenity set means no pool for off-day swimming

reddit_quotes.quote: Condo walls are very soundproof. Can barely hear cars on the highway or neighbours. Lived here 7 months, condo management responds quickly.
Lived at The Yards for 5 years and thoroughly enjoy it. Gym has full weights and a squat rack.

headline: TEN93 Queen West (Pemberton 2018) — boutique west-end pick
why_top: Pemberton + 2018 build (newer than most picks). Boutique scale. Walkable to Trinity Bellwoods, Queen W, Ossington. NOT Urbancorp (unlike its Little Portugal neighbors). Dovercourt corridor is creative-class indie, real cafés.
watch_out: Slightly LONGER drive to CVH than Liberty buildings (~3 min). Queen W safety zone proximity (City of Toronto declared Community Safety Zone Sept 2024 — Queen+Sudbury area). But Dovercourt is the SAFER edge.

building.name: TEN93 Queen West
building.amenities: Concierge, gym, rooftop deck/garden, BBQs, party/meeting room, bike storage, visitor parking, guest suites
red_flags: High maintenance fees (~$0.92/sqft) — for a renter, this gets baked into rent
Only 134 units = limited concierge hours possible
RAW Design floorplates can be quirky

headline: The Carnaby (Streetcar 2016) — boutique 213-unit West Queen W
why_top: Streetcar Developments = credible local dev with decent track record. 213 units = boutique scale without mega-tower issues. 2016 vintage. Closer to Ossington nightlife than most picks (walking distance to Ossington strip).
watch_out: Queen W shootout context (Nov 11, 2024 at 1196 Queen) — the building is 1 block from that incident. Verify daytime + nighttime walk safety yourself. Streetcar dev is solid; building itself is fine.

building.name: The Carnaby (Phase 1)
building.amenities: Outdoor terrace, state-of-art fitness centre, indoor party lounge, guest suites, adjacent urban park
red_flags: Proximity to Nov 2024 Queen W shootout
Lane/laneway address means orientation/sun varies wildly by unit
